heading: Notice of country of origin
label: 1638a Notice of country of origin

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: 1 Requirement
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (b), a retailer of a covered commodity shall inform consumers, at the final point of sale of the covered commodity to consumers, of the country of origin of the covered commodity.

children.heading: Designation of country of origin for lamb, chicken, goat, and venison meat
children.label: 2 Designation of country of origin for lamb, chicken, goat, and venison meat

children.heading: United States country of origin
children.label: A United States country of origin
i
children.text: exclusively born, raised, and slaughtered in the United States;

children.label: ii
children.text: born and raised in Alaska or Hawaii and transported for a period of not more than 60 days through Canada to the United States and slaughtered in the United States; or

children.label: iii
children.text: present in the United States on or before , and once present in the United States, remained continuously in the United States.July 15, 2008

children.label: I
children.text: not exclusively born, raised, and slaughtered in the United States,

children.label: II
children.text: born, raised, or slaughtered in the United States, and

children.label: III
children.text: not imported into the United States for immediate slaughter,

children.heading: Relation to general requirement
children.label: ii Relation to general requirement
children.text: Nothing in this subparagraph alters the mandatory requirement to inform consumers of the country of origin of covered commodities under paragraph (1).

children.label: i
children.text: the country from which the animal was imported; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the United States.

children.heading: Foreign country of origin
children.label: D Foreign country of origin
children.text: A retailer of a covered commodity that is lamb, chicken, goat, or venison meat that is derived from an animal that is not born, raised, or slaughtered in the United States shall designate a country other than the United States as the country of origin of such commodity.

children.heading: Ground lamb, chicken, goat, and venison
children.label: E Ground lamb, chicken, goat, and venison
i
children.text: a list of all countries of origin of such ground lamb, ground chicken, ground goat, or ground venison; or

children.label: ii
children.text: a list of all reasonably possible countries of origin of such ground lamb, ground chicken, ground goat, or ground venison.

children.heading: Designation of country of origin for fish
children.label: 3 Designation of country of origin for fish
i
children.text: in the case of farm-raised fish, is hatched, raised, harvested, and processed in the United States; and

children.label: I
children.text: harvested in the United States, a territory of the United States, or a State, or by a vessel that is documented under chapter 121 of title 46 or registered in the United States; and

children.label: II
children.text: processed in the United States, a territory of the United States, or a State, including the waters thereof, or aboard a vessel that is documented under chapter 121 of title 46 or registered in the United States.

children.heading: Designation of wild fish and farm-raised fish
children.label: B Designation of wild fish and farm-raised fish
children.text: The notice of country of origin for wild fish and farm-raised fish shall distinguish between wild fish and farm-raised fish.

children.heading: Designation of country of origin for perishable agricultural commodities, ginseng, peanuts, pecans, and macadamia nuts
children.label: 4 Designation of country of origin for perishable agricultural commodities, ginseng, peanuts, pecans, and macadamia nuts

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A retailer of a covered commodity that is a perishable agricultural commodity, ginseng, peanut, pecan, or macadamia nut may designate the covered commodity as having a United States country of origin only if the covered commodity is exclusively produced in the United States.

children.heading: State, region, locality of the United States
children.label: B State, region, locality of the United States
children.text: With respect to a covered commodity that is a perishable agricultural commodity, ginseng, peanut, pecan, or macadamia nut produced exclusively in the United States, designation by a retailer of the State, region, or locality of the United States where such commodity was produced shall be sufficient to identify the United States as the country of origin.

children.heading: Exemption for food service establishments
children.label: b Exemption for food service establishments

children.text: prepared or served in a food service establishment; and

children.label: A
children.text: offered for sale or sold at the food service establishment in normal retail quantities; or

children.label: B
children.text: served to consumers at the food service establishment.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The information required by subsection (a) may be provided to consumers by means of a label, stamp, mark, placard, or other clear and visible sign on the covered commodity or on the package, display, holding unit, or bin containing the commodity at the final point of sale to consumers.

children.heading: Labeled commodities
children.label: 2 Labeled commodities
children.text: If the covered commodity is already individually labeled for retail sale regarding country of origin, the retailer shall not be required to provide any additional information to comply with this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary may conduct an audit of any person that prepares, stores, handles, or distributes a covered commodity for retail sale to verify compliance with this subchapter (including the regulations promulgated under ).section 1638c(b) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A person subject to an audit under paragraph (1) shall provide the Secretary with verification of the country of origin of covered commodities. Records maintained in the course of the normal conduct of the business of such person, including animal health papers, import or customs documents, or producer affidavits, may serve as such verification.

children.heading: Prohibition on requirement of additional records
children.label: B Prohibition on requirement of additional records
children.text: The Secretary may not require a person that prepares, stores, handles, or distributes a covered commodity to maintain a record of the country of origin of a covered commodity other than those maintained in the course of the normal conduct of the business of such person.

children.heading: Information
children.label: e Information
children.text: Any person engaged in the business of supplying a covered commodity to a retailer shall provide information to the retailer indicating the country of origin of the covered commodity.

children.heading: Mandatory identification
children.label: 1 Mandatory identification
children.text: The Secretary shall not use a mandatory identification system to verify the country of origin of a covered commodity.

children.label: A
children.text: the carcass grading and certification system carried out under this Act;

children.label: B
children.text: the origin verification system established to carry out the child and adult care food program established under ; orsection 1766 of title 42

children.label: C
children.text: the origin verification system established to carry out the market access program under .section 5623(b) of this title
